tools.name: list_apps
tools.description: List your published apps on FreeAppStore. Requires authentication (connect with a FAS session token).

tools.name: deploy_status
tools.description: Check the deploy status of an app (last 5 GitHub Actions runs). No auth needed for public repos.

tools.name: app_info
tools.description: Get info about any app on FreeAppStore — live URL, repo, store listing.

tools.name: app_logs
tools.description: Query recent logs for an app — errors, warnings, SDK calls, build info. Requires authentication (app owner).

tools.name: platform_guide
tools.description: Get the FreeAppStore platform guide (SKILLS.md) for AI-assisted development. Returns the full guide that tells you how to build apps on the platform.

tools.name: sdk_reference
tools.description: Quick reference for @freeappstore/sdk — imports, features, and usage patterns for auth, KV, counters, collections, rooms, proxy, hooks, and UI components.

tools.name: create_app
tools.description: Create AND publish a brand-new app on FreeAppStore, end to end. Provisions the GitHub repo + R2 hosting + store listing (same as `fas publish`), scaffolds the chosen template, and pushes it so the app deploys live at <app_id>.freeappstore.online (~1-2 min). Then use read_file/update_files to build i

tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List the files in an app's repo (so you know what to read/edit).

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read one file's contents from an app's repo (e.g. web/src/App.tsx).

tools.name: agent_build
tools.description: Hand a natural-language prompt to the FreeAppStore VibeCode AGENT — the platform's own AI writes the code AND deploys it. This is different from create_app/update_files (where the CALLING model writes the code): here you just prompt, and the platform builds. Uses your stored AI key (provider must be

tools.name: agent_status
tools.description: Check a VibeCode agent build session (started with agent_build): the app id it's building, deploy phase, and live URL once ready.

tools.name: update_files
tools.description: Improve an app you own: write/overwrite one or more files in its repo with full new contents. The push auto-deploys to <app_id>.freeappstore.online in ~30-60s. Requires authentication + ownership.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "list_apps" references sensitive artifacts (session token). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: List your published apps on FreeAppStore. Requires authentication (connect with a FAS session token).

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "list_files" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "read_file" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: app_info, agent_build, agent_status · Private data: list_apps, list_files, read_file, agent_build, update_files · Public sink: create_app · Destructive: update_files.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: app_info, agent_build, agent_status · Private data: list_apps, list_files, read_file, agent_build, update_files · Public sink: create_app · Destructive: update_files

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
